perturbation.original_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, and at the time of its disclosure, the Disclosing Party's identify such information to be protected in accordance with t his Agreement ( "Confidential Information" ), shall be considered confidenti al and restricted property of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, regardless of whether the Disclosing Party identifies such information to be protected in accordance with this Agreement ("Confidential Information"), shall be considered confidential and restricted property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for verbal disclosures to be explicitly identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, the contract broadens the scope of 'Confidential Information' beyond what is typically protected under trade secret laws, creating potential for overreach and unenforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: The UTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy. Not requiring verbal disclosures to be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure could be argued as a failure to take reasonable measures to protect the information, thus undermining its status as a trade secret.

perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the requirement for verbal disclosures to be explicitly identified as confidential at the time of disclosure. The LLM-generated Law Explanation states that the UTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy, and not requiring verbal disclosures to be identified as confidential could be argued as a failure to take reasonable measures to protect the information. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is empty and Snippet 2 is a 'Page Not Found' error, the Perturbation Explanation, Law Explanation, and the textual change itself, combined with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction', support the determination that the modified text contradicts the obligation to take reasonable measures to maintain secrecy, potentially undermining its status as a trade secret. The change broadens the scope of 'Confidential Information' beyond what is typically protected under trade secret laws.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , and being subject to the rules of intellectual property.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party, either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party, regardless of whether it is subject to the rules of intellectual property.
perturbation.explanation: By stating 'regardless of whether it is subject to the rules of intellectual property,' the modified text attempts to protect information that might not qualify for IP protection (e.g., ideas not yet developed, information in the public domain), potentially conflicting with established IP laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Copyright Act protects original works of authorship fixed in a tangible medium. The modification attempts to extend confidentiality to information regardless of whether it meets the criteria for copyright protection (originality, fixation), potentially creating conflict with copyright law principles by restricting access to non-copyrightable material.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 U.S. Code § 102 - Subject matter of copyright: In general | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 17 U.S. Code § 102 - Subject matter of copyright: In general U.S. Code Notes prev | next (a) Copyright protection subsists, in accordance with this title, in original works of authorship fixed in any tangible medium of expression, now known or later developed, from which they can be perceived, reproduced, or otherwise communicated, either directly or with the aid of a machine or device. Works of authorship include the following categories: (1) literary works ; (2) musical works, including any accompanying words; (3) dramatic works, including any accompanying music; (4) pantomimes and choreographic works; (5) pictorial, graphic, and sculptural works; (6) motion pictures and other audiovisual works ; (7) sound recordings ; and (8) architectural works . (b) In no case does copyright protection for an original work of authorship extend to any idea, procedure, process , system, method of operation, concept, principle, or discovery, regardless of the form in which it is described, explained, illustrated, or embodied in such work. ( Pub. L. 94–553, title I, § 101 , Oct. 19, 1976 , 90 Stat. 2544 ; Pub. L. 101–650, title VII, § 703 , Dec. 1, 1990 , 104 Stat. 5133 .) Historical and Revision Notes house report no. 94–1476 Original Works of Authorship. The two fundamental criteria of copyright protection—originality and fixation in tangible form are restated in the first sentence of this cornerstone provision. The phrase “original works or authorship,” which is purposely left undefined, is intended to incorporate without change the standard of originality established by the courts under the present copyright statute. This standard does not include requirements of novelty, ingenuity, or esthetic merit, and there is no intention to enlarge the standard of copyright protection to require them
perturbation.justification: The modified text broadens the scope of confidentiality beyond information that qualifies for intellectual property protection. This contradicts the Copyright Act, which, as Snippet 2 states, protects "original works of authorship fixed in any tangible medium of expression". By attempting to protect information regardless of its IP status, the changed text conflicts with the principle that ideas, procedures, and concepts not fixed in a tangible medium are not copyrightable and thus are not subject to copyright protection. Therefore, extending confidentiality to non-copyrightable material creates a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall no t constitute a wai ver of any term.
perturbation.changed_text: Failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement at any point in time shall constitute a permanent waiver of that term.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts standard contract law principles regarding waiver. A consistent failure to enforce a provision can imply a waiver, but a single instance usually does not. This attempts to create an automatic and permanent waiver, which is legally problematic.
perturbation.law_explanation: Under general contract law principles (reflected in the Restatement (Second) of Contracts), waiver typically requires a voluntary and intentional relinquishment of a known right. This modification attempts to create a waiver based solely on a single instance of non-enforcement, regardless of intent, which contradicts the established legal understanding of waiver.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: waiver | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you waiver A waiver is the intentional or voluntary relinquishment or surrender of a recognized right or privilege . In legal contexts, a waiver is often documented in writing, but it can also be implied through actions . By waiving a right, an individual or party chooses not to enforce a particular right, claim , or provision they are legally entitled to. perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction by stating that a single failure to enforce a provision constitutes a permanent waiver. This contradicts the principle that waiver requires intentional relinquishment of a known right, as highlighted in Scraped Law Snippet 1: 'A waiver is the intentional or voluntary relinquishment or surrender of a recognized right or privilege.' The modified text attempts to create a waiver automatically, regardless of intent, which is a direct contradiction of established legal understanding.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be in force on the Execution Date and shall remain in force for a period of twenty-four (24) months. Notwithstanding the term of the Agreement , the Parties undertake to maintain the confidentiality obligation for a period of three (3) years after Confidential Information disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be in force on the Execution Date and shall remain in force perpetually. Notwithstanding the term of the Agreement, the Parties undertake to maintain the confidentiality obligation perpetually after Confidential Information disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This modification makes the agreement perpetual, attempting to bind the parties indefinitely. While NDAs can extend beyond the initial term, making them perpetual is generally disfavored and can be challenged as an unreasonable restraint of trade, especially under certain state laws that limit the duration of non-compete and confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code § 16600 generally prohibits contracts that restrain a person from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business. While this statute primarily targets non-compete agreements, a perpetual confidentiality obligation could be viewed as an unreasonable restraint if it unduly restricts a party's ability to work or conduct business based on information learned during the agreement's term.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 16600 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 7. GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 1. Contracts in Restraint of Trade [16600 - 16607] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) 16600. (a) Except as provided in this chapter, every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void. (b) (1) This section shall be read broadly, in accordance with Edwards v. Arthur Andersen LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, to void the application of any noncompete agreement in an employment context, or any noncompete clause in an employment contract, no matter how narrowly tailored, that does not satisfy an exception in this chapter. (2) This subdivision does not constitute a change in, but is declaratory of, existing law. (c) This section shall not be limited to contracts where the person being restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business is a party to the contract. (Amended by Stats. 2023, Ch. 828, Sec. 1. (AB 1076) Effective January 1, 2024.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the confidentiality obligation to be 'perpetual,' while the original text had a defined term. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation suggest that such a perpetual obligation could be viewed as an unreasonable restraint of trade under California Business and Professions Code § 16600. Scraped Law Snippet 1, specifically BPC 16600 (a), states that 'every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void.' A perpetual confidentiality agreement could be interpreted as such a restraint, creating a legal contradiction because it potentially violates this statute.
